title: Coherent-constructible correspondence and homological mirror symmetry I
speaker: Bohan Fang

proof_stages.label: Toric setup and Morelli's \(K\)-theoretic shadow
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes fan and lattice notation for a smooth complete projective toric variety, recalls toric divisors and quotient coordinates, and motivates the coherent-constructible correspondence by Morelli's injection from equivariant \(K\)-theory into piecewise-constant functions on \(M_{\mathbb R}\).

proof_stages.label: Categorified correspondence via theta generators
proof_stages.summary: The coherent-constructible correspondence is stated as a quasi-fully faithful DG functor from equivariant coherent sheaves to constructible sheaves on \(M_{\mathbb R}\), and its proof begins by matching quasi-coherent theta objects on affine toric charts with polyhedral constructible theta sheaves through identical Hom calculations.

proof_stages.label: Passage from quasi-coherent generators to coherent sheaves
proof_stages.summary: Using Čech resolutions by top-dimensional affine toric charts, vector bundles and hence coherent sheaves are shown to lie in the DG category generated by the theta-prime objects, completing the embedding side of the correspondence.

proof_stages.label: Microlocal characterization of the essential image
proof_stages.summary: The image of coherent sheaves is characterized as compactly supported constructible sheaves whose singular support is contained in the torically defined conical Lagrangian \(\Lambda_\Sigma\subset T^*M_{\mathbb R}\), with \(\Lambda_\Sigma\) constructed from the cones of the fan and lattice translates.

proof_stages.label: Mirror-symmetric reformulation through constructible sheaves
proof_stages.summary: The speaker interprets the CCC as a version of homological mirror symmetry by invoking the Nadler-Zaslow equivalence between constructible sheaves and a wrapped Fukaya category of a cotangent bundle, producing a Fukaya-category model with asymptotic behavior controlled by \(\Lambda_\Sigma\) and, after forgetting equivariance, a quasi-embedding into the cotangent bundle of a real torus.

proof_stages.label: Nadler-Zaslow Fukaya model and standard objects
proof_stages.summary: The relevant unwrapped Fukaya category is described by exact possibly noncompact Lagrangians, morphisms via small geodesic-flow perturbations, and the correspondence sending standard and costandard constructible sheaves to standard and costandard Lagrangians.

proof_stages.label: T-duality comparison for toric line bundles
proof_stages.summary: After deleting the anticanonical divisor to obtain the torus fibration and its dual, a torus-invariant Hermitian metric on a line bundle is used to build a graph Lagrangian whose moment-map image is the corresponding polytope, and for ample line bundles this T-dual Lagrangian is identified in the Fukaya category with the standard Lagrangian obtained from the CCC plus microlocalization.
